name: Onpath
description_short: Federal credit union headquartered in Metairie, LA with $1.13B in assets, 87,350 members, and 15 branches offering deposit accounts, lending, and financial services.
description_long:
Onpath Federal Credit Union is a federal credit union that has served its membership for 65 years since opening in 1961. Headquartered in Metairie, Louisiana, the institution has grown to manage $1.13 billion in total assets across 15 branch locations with approximately 87,350 members and 248 employees. The credit union maintains a federal charter (number 14692) and serves a multiple common bond membership, primarily composed of federal, state, and local government employees.

Onpath provides comprehensive financial services typical of a mid-sized credit union, including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card programs, credit builder loans, first-time homebuyer programs, and low-cost wire transfer services. The institution holds significant lending portfolios with $909.1 million in total loans outstanding, including $483.8 million in real estate loans, $134.8 million in unsecured loans, and $288.4 million in other loans. Members also have access to international remittance services, demonstrating the credit union's commitment to serving diverse populations.

Onpath distinguishes itself through its government employee focus and community-oriented membership model. As an FHLB member institution with a last audit date of December 1, 2024, the credit union maintains regulatory compliance and safety oversight. The organization is led by CEO Jared Freeman and Chairperson Martha Castillo, and reported net income of $5.2 million in the most recent reporting period, indicating financial stability. The credit union's equity position of $102.4 million and capital reserves demonstrate financial strength relative to its asset base.

While Onpath offers solid core banking services and maintains financial stability, it operates as a traditional community credit union without the digital-first features or extensive product breadth of larger national institutions or online banks. The membership restriction to government employees and related common bonds may limit accessibility for general consumers. Prospective members should verify their eligibility under the credit union's membership requirements before attempting to join. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: 65-year operating history with $1.13 billion in assets and strong financial stability (2024 audit completed)
Multiple common bond membership eligibility focused on federal, state, and local government employees
Competitive credit union services including credit builder loans and first-time homebuyer programs
15 branch locations across Louisiana for in-person service and local community presence
International remittance services and low-cost wire transfers for global financial needs
Strong capital position with $102.4 million in equity and positive $5.2 million annual net income
FHLB member institution with regulatory oversight and NCUSIF deposit insurance protection
cons: Membership restricted to federal, state, local government employees and related common bonds—not open to general public
Limited digital banking innovation compared to fintech competitors or larger national credit unions
Branch network limited to Louisiana geography, reducing accessibility for members outside the state
No mention of mobile banking features, online account opening, or advanced digital services on profile
Mid-sized institution may lack product depth compared to major banks (no investment services listed beyond basic banking)
best_for: Federal, state, and local government employees seeking community-based credit union banking
Government workers wanting credit builder loans and first-time homebuyer program support
Louisiana residents needing in-person banking through multiple convenient branch locations
Individuals with international financial needs requiring remittance and wire transfer services
diagnosis: Onpath Federal Credit Union is best for Louisiana-based government employees seeking traditional credit union banking with personalized service, credit-building products, and community connection. The main caveat is membership eligibility—this credit union is not open to the general public and requires membership in government employee or related groups, making it inaccessible for most consumers unless they meet specific employment criteria.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
